subject: Gastroenterology
question: A 65-year-old man presents with hematemesis and melena. BP 90/60, HR 115. What is the MOST important initial step?

options.text: Establish 2 large-bore IVs, start crystalloid resuscitation
options.explanation: Hemodynamic stabilization is priority in acute UGIB. 2 large-bore (18G) IVs, crystalloid bolus, type and crossmatch blood. Then IV PPI (esomeprazole 80mg bolus + infusion). Endoscopy within 24h after stabilization.
Endoscopy should be performed AFTER hemodynamic stabilization. Unstable patient going to endoscopy risks aspiration and cardiac arrest. Resuscitate first.
Octreotide is for VARICEAL bleeding (splanchnic vasoconstriction). Not all UGIB is variceal. Start after endoscopy confirms varices. PPIs and fluids first.
NG lavage has limited diagnostic/therapeutic value in UGIB. Not routinely recommended. May be used to clear stomach before endoscopy but not initial priority.
Surgery reserved for refractory bleeding despite endoscopic and interventional radiology approaches. Not first-line. Resuscitate and scope first.

educationalObjective: Prioritize hemodynamic resuscitation in acute UGIB before diagnostic/therapeutic interventions.
keyTakeaway: UGIB: 2 large-bore IVs → fluids → transfuse if needed → IV PPI → endoscopy within 24h. Glasgow-Blatchford score for risk stratification.
